name: Alfredo Antonini
abstract: Alfredo Antonini (May 31, 1901 – November 3, 1983) was a leading Italian-American symphony conductor and composer who was active on the international concert stage as well as on the CBS radio and television networks from the 1930s through the early 1970s. In 1972 he received an Emmy Award for Outstanding Achievement in Religious Programm…

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: he recommended me to Alfredo Antonini and Vincent Zorrie. They were the conductor and piano player at WRNS radio station. And I auditioned there and I got the job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Oneonta, New York, December 28, 2005

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Do you remember the Tuesday night -- the show they used to do with Alfredo Antonini, that was a forty-piece band just for radio, all staff musicians at CBS.
